name: Railsr (Railsbank) Embedded Finance API
what_it_does: Railsr (formerly Railsbank) embedded finance REST API for banking-as-a-service and embedded finance platform. Enables AI agents to manage customer and end-user onboarding with KYC/AML verification, handle virtual and physical card issuance and management automation, access real-time ledger and transaction data for embedded wallet management, retrieve payment processing and money movement for domestic and international transfers, manage card program configuration and spend controls, handle FX and multi-currency account management, access compliance and regulatory reporting data, retrieve fraud monitoring and transaction screening results, manage virtual account and IBAN provisioning, and integrate embedded finance capabilities with consumer apps, enterprise expense platforms, and marketplace payment systems.
use_cases: Issuing virtual cards for expense management from FinOps agents
Managing embedded wallets and ledger entries from marketplace agents
Automating KYC/AML onboarding from financial compliance agents
Processing payments and FX from embedded finance agents
not_for: Traditional retail banking without embedded finance or BaaS context
Consumer personal banking without enterprise embedded finance use case
Non-financial product builders without card issuance or payment needs
best_when: A fintech or enterprise embedding financial services wants AI agents to automate card issuance, ledger management, customer onboarding, payment processing, and compliance workflows within their product.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Card issuance and payment automation carries fraud liability — automated spend controls and velocity limits are required before enabling card programs. Customer onboarding automation must complete KYC/AML screening before financial services activation. Multi-currency transactions without FX rate validation can cause significant financial losses.

auth.notes: Railsr uses API key authentication for server-to-server and OAuth 2.0 for end-user-facing flows. Sandbox environment at sandbox.railsr.com. Webhooks for real-time transaction and card event notifications. UK FCA regulated. EU PSD2 compliant. Documentation at docs.railsr.com. Separate production and sandbox API keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox access for development; production requires partnership agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Transaction-based pricing plus platform fee; card program and ledger modules separately priced
pricing.notes: London, United Kingdom. Founded 2016. Rebranded Railsbank to Railsr (2022). Private company. Embedded finance and BaaS market. FCA regulated in UK, EMI license in EU. Customers include Coinbase UK card program. Financial difficulties in 2022-2023 led to restructuring. Competes with Modulr, Marqeta, and Solaris for European embedded finance.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in partnership agreement

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Card issuance and payment automation requires spend controls and velocity limits before production; insufficient controls create fraud liability
Company stability — Railsr faced financial difficulties in 2022-2023 and underwent restructuring; verify current operational status before building production dependencies
UK FCA regulated — operating a card program requires either own FCA authorization or operating under Railsr's regulatory umbrella; legal review required
Idempotency keys — use idempotency keys for all payment operations to prevent duplicate transactions on network timeout
KYC/AML required — customer onboarding requires KYC completion before financial services activation; automate screening but require human review for edge cases
Webhook reliability — implement idempotent webhook handlers; duplicate events possible; track event IDs to prevent duplicate processing
agent_readiness.security_notes: Embedded finance and BaaS. FCA, PSD2, GDPR, AML5D. API key/OAuth2. EU/UK. Card, payment, and ledger transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Embedded finance. FCA, PSD2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. EU/UK. Payment and card transaction data.
